title: Estate planning
description: Practice your understanding of key estate planning terms, including wills, beneficiary designation, and guardianship of minor children.

assessment_items.question_content:
Guardianship, wills, and beneficiary designation are important parts of estate planning.

 **What is one reason why guardianship of a minor child is important?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to decide who will care for a child if their parents can't
to make sure a child always has a babysitter
to make sure a child is safe

assessment_items.question_content:
Guardianship, wills, and beneficiary designation are important parts of estate planning.

 **What does *guardianship of a minor child* do in an estate plan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: it names a person whose responsibility will be to take care of a minor child
it names a person who will hold all the property until the child turns 18
it names the child as a beneficiary

assessment_items.question_content:
Guardianship, wills, and beneficiary designation are important parts of estate planning.

 **What happens if an estate plan does *not* have *guardianship of a minor child* in it?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: we will not know whose responsibility it is to take care of a minor child
we will not know who will hold all the property until the child turns 18
we will not know the beneficiary of the estate

assessment_items.question_content:
A will is always included as an essential part of estate planning.

**Why is having a will important?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to help you plan your budget and spending
to ensure your wishes are followed after you pass away
to make sure you can speak and act freely

assessment_items.question_content:
A will is always included as an essential part of estate planning.

**What does a *will* do in an estate plan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: it organizes a person's medical history
it decides who gets  a person's property after they pass away
it shares the news of a person's death

assessment_items.question_content:
A will is an essential part of estate planning.

**What happens if the estate plan does *not* have a will in it?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: we will not know the cause of someone's death
we will not know who gets  a person's property after they pass away
we will know when and where the person is getting buried

assessment_items.question_content:
Estate planning always includes a beneficiary designation as one of the key parts of the plan.

**What is the purpose of beneficiary designation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to name who will receive money from your financial accounts and assets after you pass away
to decide who gets to make medical decision for you
to make sure your debts are paid off

assessment_items.question_content:
Estate planning always includes a beneficiary designation as one of the key parts of the plan.

**What does the *beneficiary designation* do?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: it names a person who will receive money from your financial accounts and assets after you pass away
it is a place where you sign your will
it names a person who will take care of any minor child(ren)

assessment_items.question_content:
Estate planning includes a beneficiary designation as one of the key parts of the plan.

**What happens if the estate plan does *not* have a *beneficiary designation*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: we will not know know who will receive money from financial accounts and any assets
we will not know which court will oversee the process
we will not know who is in charge of carrying out the final wishes

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the following scenario using key estate  planning terms:**

Sarah is a single mom with two kids, Jack and Maya. Sarah writes a [[☃ dropdown 1]] where she leaves her house to Jack and Maya, and her favorite necklace to her best friend, Liliana. She names her sister Kara a(n) [[☃ dropdown 2]] of her kids because she knows she will love them and care for them.
hints: Estate planning is the process of arranging and managing your money, property, and other belongings for the future.

There are two types of estate documents: a will and a trust. 

A **will** is a legal *document* that says who gets your stuff when you die.

A **trust** is *arrangement* where someone holds your property for the benefit of others.

There are three kinds of estate persons: an executor, a guardian, and a healthcare proxy.

An **executor** is the person who makes sure your will is followed.

A **guardian** is person who takes care of your kids if you can't.

A **healthcare proxy** is someone who makes medical decisions for you if you can't.

Use these definitions to help you fill out the correct terms for the fill in the blanks.

Here is the correct answer:

Sarah writes a **will** where she leaves her house to Jack and Maya, and her favorite necklace to her best friend, Liliana. She names her sister Kara a **guardian** of her kids because she knows she will love them and care for them.

choices.content: trust
will
guardian
executor
guardian
probate

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the following scenario using key estate  planning terms:**

Jaq and Amelia have a daughter named Elle, who is $3$ years old. They want to make sure Elle is taken care of if anything happens to them, and they want their cousin, Roberto to take care of Elle's needs until she turns $18$. So, they set up a [[☃ dropdown 1]] to allow Roberto to provide for Elle and hold all the property for Elle, until she becomes an adult. In their will and trust, they named Elle as the [[☃ dropdown 2]].
hints: Estate planning is the process of arranging and managing your money, property, and other belongings for the future.

There are two types of estate documents: a will and a trust. 

A **will** is a legal *document* that says who gets your stuff when you die.

A **trust** is *arrangement* where someone holds your property for the benefit of others.

There are three kinds of estate persons: an executor, a guardian, and a healthcare proxy.

An **executor** is the person who makes sure your will is followed.

A **guardian** is person who takes care of your kids if you can't.

A **beneficiary**: The *person* who gets your stuff from your will, trust, or life insurance.

Use these definitions to help you fill out the correct terms for the fill in the blanks.

Here is the correct answer:

Jaq and Amelia have a daughter named Elle, who is $3$ years old. They want to make sure Elle is taken care of if anything happens to them, and they want their cousin, Roberto to take care of Elle's needs until she turns $18$. So, they set up a **trust** to allow Roberto to provide for Elle and hold all the property for her, until she becomes an adult. In their will and trust, they named Elle as the **beneficiary**.

choices.content: will
trust
beneficiary
beneficiary
guardian
executor

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the following scenario using key estate  planning terms:**

Tya has an uncle who loves helping and volunteering in his community. He wrote a [[☃ dropdown 1]] where he directs that after his passing all his money and property go to the local refugee center. He also shared that he would like to have his organs donated in case of his death, so he made Tya his  [[☃ dropdown 2]] to ensure doctors and hospitals are aware of his wishes.
hints: Estate planning is the process of arranging and managing your money, property, and other belongings for the future.

There are two types of estate documents: a will and a trust. 

A **will** is a legal *document* that says who gets your stuff when you die.

A **trust** is *arrangement* where someone holds your property for the benefit of others.

There are three kinds of estate persons: an executor, a guardian, and a healthcare proxy.

An **executor** is the person who makes sure your will is followed.

A **healthcare proxy** is someone who makes medical decisions for you if you can't.

A **beneficiary**: The *person* who gets your stuff from your will, trust, or life insurance.

Use these definitions to help you fill out the correct terms for the fill in the blanks.

Here is the correct answer:

Tya has an uncle who loves helping and volunteering in his community. He wrote a **will** where he directs that after his passing all his money and property go to the local refugee center. He also shared that he would like to have his organs donated in case of his death, so he made Tya his  **healthcare proxy** to ensure doctors and hospitals are aware of his wishes.

choices.content: will
trust
probate
beneficiary
executor
healthcare proxy
